1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

10	/-!
11	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
12	
13	This file is **Stratum 4** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
14	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
15	
16	* the chart-bridge `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (iter-173 `chart-bridge173` recipe step (a));
17	* the per-chart ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` of the scaling action;
18	* the pullback cover `gmScalingP1_cover` of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`;
19	* the per-chart scheme morphism `gmScalingP1_chart`, the cocycle agreement
20	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`, and the over-coherence
21	  `gmScalingP1_over_coherence`;
22	* the bare scaling morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar`;
23	* the load-bearing fixed-point property `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`;
24	* the product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m` exported for Lane B in
25	  `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.
26	
27	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`.
28	-/

30	set_option autoImplicit false
31	set_option linter.style.setOption false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	noncomputable section
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
42	
43	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
44	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
45	definition). -/
46	
47	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
48	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
49	
50	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
51	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
52	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
53	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
54	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
55	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
56	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
57	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
58	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
59	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
60	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
61	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
62	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
63	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
67	
68	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
69	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
70	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
71	
72	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
73	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
74	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
75	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
76	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
77	
78	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
79	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
80	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
81	
82	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
83	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
84	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
85	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
86	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
87	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
88	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
89	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
90	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
91	
92	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
93	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
94	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
95	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
96	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
97	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
98	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
99	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
100	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
101	        GmRing kbar))
102	
103	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
104	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
105	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
106	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
108	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
109	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
110	
111	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
112	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
113	
114	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
115	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
116	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
117	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
118	
119	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
120	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
121	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
122	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
123	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
124	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
125	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
126	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
127	        (TensorProduct kbar

258	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
259	Lane B helper, helper budget = 1).
260	
261	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
262	
263	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
264	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
265	
266	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
267	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
268	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
269	satisfy on the intersection:
270	
271	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
272	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
273	
274	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
275	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
276	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
277	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
278	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.
279	
280	**Status (iter-181):** honest direct sorry. The formal proof requires:
281	
282	1. Identification of `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1)` with
283	   `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)` via Mathlib's
284	   `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul`
285	   (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:883`).
286	2. A ring-level computation in `Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing` using
287	   `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf` (= `t · t⁻¹ = 1`) and
288	   `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul`.

473	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :

482	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by

486	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
487	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
488	          (Localization.Away
489	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
490	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
491	      intro x y h

12	## Question
13

15	`Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)` intersection-ring identification for
16	`ProjectiveLineBar kbar = Proj ⟨k̄[X 0, X 1]⟩`.

19	2. **Route 3** — `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` `iotaGm_range_isOpen` (L98).

24	  helper.

33	## Decisions identified
34	
35	### Decision 1: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul` is in Mathlib and is the right idiom
36	
37	- **Mathlib idiom**: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul`
38	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:883`)
39	  packages: for `f ∈ 𝒜 d` (d ≠ 0) and `g ∈ 𝒜 e` and `x = f * g`,
40	  `Away 𝒜 x` is the localization of `Away 𝒜 f` at the image
41	  `Away.isLocalizationElem hf hg = g^d / f^e ∈ Away 𝒜 f` (L877).
42	- **Project's current path**: the iter-181 Lane B task_result correctly
43	  identified this lemma (lines 67–68 of the report) but treated it as a
44	  stand-alone Mathlib bridge to be threaded by hand.
45	- **Gap**: identical (the algebraic content is shipped).
46	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with `Away.isLocalization_mul`. No project
47	  helper is needed at the ring level — Decision 2 supersedes the ring
48	  step entirely.
49	
50	### Decision 2 (the load-bearing one): Mathlib ALREADY ships the Spec-level intersection iso `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
51	
52	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
53	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:258-265`)
54	  is verbatim:
55	
56	  ```lean
57	  noncomputable
58	  def pullbackAwayιIso :
59	      Limits.pullback (awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm) (awayι 𝒜 g g_deg hm') ≅
60	        Spec (.of <| Away 𝒜 x) :=  -- where hx : x = f * g
61	    IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq ...
62	  ```
63	
64	  Companion `reassoc (attr := simp)` lemmas (L268-302):
65	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι` (L268): `iso.hom ≫ awayι 𝒜 x _ _ =
66	    pullback.fst _ _ ≫ awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm`.
67	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left` (L275): `iso.hom ≫
68	    Spec.map (ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) = pullback.fst _ _`.
69	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_right` (L282): `iso.hom ≫
70	    Spec.map (ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 f_deg ...)) = pullback.snd _ _`.
71	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd` (L291-301):
72	    the inverse projections in terms of `Spec.map (awayMap ...)`.
73	
74	  Hover-verified end-to-end at iter-182 via `lean_run_code` (signatures
75	  reproduce identically; instances all resolve).
76	
77	- **Project's current path**: iter-181 Lane B task_result claimed
78	  "the bridge `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≅ Spec (...)` is not
79	  packaged in Mathlib as a single lemma" (report line 64). This is
80	  **incorrect at the inner pullback level** — Mathlib ships the iso
81	  `pullback (awayι X_0) (awayι X_1) ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1))` plus
82	  three `simp` lemmas that pin every projection. The project's missing
83	  step is the **pasting** that pushes this inner iso through the
84	  pullback over `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).left = pullback PLB.hom Gm.hom`.
85	
86	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (one iter of investigative dead-end
87	  attempts on `cancel_mono` / `unfold + simp` because the project
88	  treated the Mathlib gap as deeper than it is).
89	
90	- **Cost of divergence (if any)**: the iter-181 task_result's "iter-182+
91	  estimate ~70-115 LOC" included ~25-40 LOC for "build the intersection
92	  ring iso explicitly" — that line is wasted work. Mathlib already
93	  ships the Spec-level iso AND the ring-level iso (via `awayMap`).
94	
95	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — discard the "build a project-side
96	  `Away_X0_X1_iso : Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ≃+* Localization.Away (chart-1 affine coord)`"
97	  plan from the iter-181 task_result step 1. Use `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
98	  + companion `simp` lemmas directly. No new project helper is needed
99	  at the inner ring level.
100	
101	### Decision 3: Pasting from inner to outer pullback uses generic Mathlib limit-pasting
102	
103	- **Setup**: the cover `gmScalingP1_cover = (projectiveLineBarAffineCover).openCover.pullback₁ q`
104	  where `q := pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom : (ℙ¹⊗Gm).left ⟶ PLB.left`. By
105	  `PreZeroHypercover.pullback₁_X` / `_f` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Hypercover.Zero:97-101`),
106	  - `(cover).X i = pullback q (awayι 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i) _ _)`
107	  - `(cover).f i = pullback.fst q (awayι 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i) _ _)`
108	
109	- **Goal**: `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.
110	
111	- **Pasting strategy** (Mathlib already ships everything):
112	  1. `pullback (pullback.fst q A) (pullback.fst q B) ≅ pullback q (pullback A B)`
113	     via two `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`s + `pullback.congrHom` on
114	     `pullback (q ≫ φ) ψ = ...` (use the Mathlib analogue of the project's
115	     `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` recipe, applied symmetrically).
116	  2. The inner `pullback (awayι X_0) (awayι X_1) ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1))`
117	     via `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (Decision 2).
118	  3. The outer `pullback q (awayι 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ≅
119	     Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)` — uniform-in-`i`
120	     version of the existing `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`, applied with
121	     `f := X 0 * X 1 ∈ 𝒜 2` (degree `m = 2`, witnessed by
122	     `MvPolynomial.IsHomogeneous.mul` of the two `isHomogeneous_X`).
123	
124	  Steps 1 and 3 mirror the existing project recipe; step 2 is one
125	  Mathlib lemma application. **No new project helper is needed at
126	  the inner ring level**; only an outer "intersection cover X iso"
127	  (mirror of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` at the X 0 * X 1 chart).
128	
129	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** for the *outer* iso
130	  `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso : pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1)
131	    ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`. ~50-60 LOC; the
132	  body is one chain of `≪≫` Mathlib isos.
133	
134	### Decision 4: chart-1 section for `iotaGm_range_isOpen` — `pullback.lift` over `pointOfVec`'s factorization through `awayι X_1`
135	
136	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Over.lift_left`
137	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/LocallyCartesianClosed/Over.lean:130-131`):
138	  `(CartesianMonoidalCategory.lift f g).left = pullback.lift f.left g.left _`.
139	  Combined with `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd`, this expresses
140	  `(lift (toUnit ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left` as the pullback-universal-property
141	  morphism into `pullback PLB.hom Gm.hom = (ℙ¹⊗Gm).left`.
142	
143	- **Section into chart-1 of cover**: `ProjectiveLineBar.onePt = pointOfVec
144	  kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 _`. `pointOfVec` is `Over.homMk
145	  (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
146	  (evalIntoGlobal kbar v) ...)`. The `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`
147	  morphism factors through ANY chart of `Proj 𝒜` where the corresponding
148	  generator is mapped to a unit — for `onePt` with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`, BOTH
149	  `X_0 ↦ 1` and `X_1 ↦ 1` are units, so `onePt.left` factors through
150	  `awayι 𝒜 (X 1) _ _` via a section `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))`
151	  (= `Spec.map` of the chart-1 ring's eval-at-1 map). The Mathlib
152	  factorization lemma is `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`
153	  (cited by the iter-168 `gmscaling-deep.md` analogist).
154	
155	- **Section formula**:
156	  ```
157	  s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1 = pullback q (awayι X_1)
158	  s := pullback.lift ((toUnit Gm ≫ onePt).left ≫ r_1.inv) (𝟙 Gm.left ≫ Gm.hom...) _
159	       -- actually: pullback.lift (Gm.left ⟶ Spec(Away X_1)) (Gm.left ⟶ Gm.left)
160	       --   with compatibility coming from the over-structure
161	  ```
162	
163	  Concretely: extract `r_1 := Spec.map (ofHom (the eval map Away 𝒜 (X 1) → k̄))`
164	  via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` on the chart-1 basic open,
165	  then `pullback.lift (toUnit_Gm ≫ r_1) (𝟙 Gm.left) (pullback compatibility)`.
166	
167	- **Open-immersion argument**: with `s` in hand,
168	  `s ≫ (cover).f 1 ≫ gmScalingP1.left = (lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1).left`
169	  by `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` and unfolding of `gmScalingP1 = Over.homMk
170	  (cover.glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart _)`. The composite
171	  `s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1` is the chart-1 morphism `(cover).X 1 ⟶
172	  ProjectiveLineBarScheme` precomposed with the section `s`. By
173	  unfolding `gmScalingP1_chart 1` (L156–175) and using
174	  `Proj.awayι` being an `IsOpenImmersion` instance
175	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic:196`), the
176	  composite is the open immersion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` mapping
177	  `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`. `IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range` closes the helper.
178	
179	- **Gap**: BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER (no single Mathlib lemma exposes the
180	  section formula generically; this is a project-specific computation).
181	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** for the chart-1 section `s`.

272	        (rfl : (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : _) 0 *
273	               (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : _) 1 = _)).hom_awayι.symm) ≪≫
274	  -- Step (c): outer iso `pullback q (awayι 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ≅
275	  --          Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗ GmRing)` —
276	  --          mirror of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` with the merged generator
277	  --          `X 0 · X 1` of degree 2.
278	  pullback.congrHom
279	    (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 2) (by norm_num)
280	      ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1)
281	      ((MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0).mul
282	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1)))
283	    (show (Gm kbar).hom =
284	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
285	  pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
286	```

288	(The `≪≫` chain is illustrative; the prover must thread the iso targets
289	to match `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι` / `pullback.fst_congrHom` exactly.
290	The point is that **every step except the `pullbackAwayιIso` step is a
291	re-application of techniques already in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`**.)

293	### Recipe 2: `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` body
294	
295	```lean
296	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
297	    pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
298	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≫
299	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (0 : Fin 2) =
300	    pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
301	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≫
302	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) := by
303	  -- Promote both sides to the intersection ring via the new iso.
304	  rw [← cancel_epi (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv]
305	  -- Each side now is `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst ≫ chart 0` (resp. snd ≫ chart 1).
306	  -- Use `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `_inv_snd` (composed through the outer iso)
307	  -- to rewrite both sides into a single Spec.map of a ring map
308	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X 0) ⊗ GmRing →+* Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing` and
309	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X 1) ⊗ GmRing →+* Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing`.
310	  simp only [Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst,
311	    Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd, ...]
312	  -- Goal now: equality of two Spec.map's. Reduce to ring equality.
313	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
314	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
315	  congr 1
316	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
317	  -- Ring-level identity: `chart 0`-side and `chart 1`-side ring maps
318	  -- agree on `Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing`. Via `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext`
319	  -- (after passing through `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`), reduce to
320	  -- evaluating on the chart-side generators `t = X 1 / X 0` (chart 0) /
321	  -- `u = X 0 / X 1` (chart 1). The compatibility `t · u = 1` in
322	  -- `Localization.Away (X 0 · X 1)` is `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf`
323	  -- applied to the merged isLocalizationElem.
324	  ext  -- or `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`
325	  -- residual algebra: `t · u = 1` (chart-product) — close via
326	  -- `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul` + `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf`.
327	  sorry  -- target: ~10 LOC
328	```

330	### Recipe 3: `iotaGm_range_isOpen` body
331	
332	```lean
333	private lemma iotaGm_range_isOpen [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
334	    IsOpen (Set.range ⇑((lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar)
335	        (𝟙 (Gm kbar))).left ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar).left)) := by
336	  -- (a) `(lift _ _).left = pullback.lift (toUnit_Gm ≫ onePt).left (𝟙 Gm.left)
337	  --    (by Over-pullback compatibility)`, via `Over.lift_left`.
338	  rw [Over.lift_left]  -- or use the `set_option` trick if defeq is tricky
339	  -- (b) `onePt.left` factors through `awayι X_1` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`.
340	  --    Extract the section `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))`.
341	  obtain ⟨r_1, h_r_1⟩ : ∃ r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
342	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
343	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X 1))),
344	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left =
345	        r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
346	          (MvPolynomial.X 1) (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos := by
347	    -- Use `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` on the chart-1 basic open
348	    -- and the fact that `evalIntoGlobal kbar (fun _ => 1)` maps `X_1 ↦ 1`
349	    -- which is a unit (so the morphism restricts to the chart).
350	    sorry  -- ~15 LOC; chart factorization via Mathlib's chart-restriction lemma
351	  -- (c) Build the section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1` via `pullback.lift`.
352	  --    `(cover).X 1 = pullback q (awayι X_1)` with `q = pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom`.
353	  --    The Gm.left-side map is `toUnit_Gm ≫ r_1`; the Gm.hom-side is `𝟙 Gm.left`.
354	  set s : (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X 1 := pullback.lift
355	    (pullback.lift ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ ... -- the PLB-side factor: factors through r_1)
356	      (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left) (...) )
357	    (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left)
358	    (...) with hs_def
359	  -- (d) `s ≫ (cover).f 1 = (lift (toUnit ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left` — pullback unique-lift.
360	  have hs_fact : s ≫ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1 =
361	      (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar)
362	        (𝟙 (Gm kbar))).left := by
363	    -- unfold `s`, apply `pullback.lift_fst`, then `Over.lift_left` rev.
364	    sorry  -- ~5 LOC
365	  -- (e) Compose with `gmScalingP1.left = (cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart _`.
366	  --    `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` gives `(cover).f 1 ≫ glueMorphisms ... = gmScalingP1_chart 1`.
367	  rw [← hs_fact, Category.assoc]
368	  change IsOpen (Set.range ⇑(s ≫ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1 ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar).left))
369	  change IsOpen (Set.range ⇑(s ≫ Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms ... -- unfold to gmScalingP1_chart 1
370	  rw [Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms (gmScalingP1_cover kbar) _ _ 1]
371	  -- (f) `s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1` is an `IsOpenImmersion`:
372	  --     the chart map factors as `s ≫ iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (ring map) ≫ Proj.awayι X_1`.
373	  --     `Proj.awayι X_1` is open-immersion (Mathlib instance); `Spec.map (ring map)` is
374	  --     open immersion iff the ring map exhibits a localization. For the chart-1 ring map
375	  --     `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` precomposed with `r_1` (eval-at-1 ⊗ id ≅ Spec.map (Gm structure)),
376	  --     this composite IS an open immersion (essentially the standard Gm ↪ 𝔸¹ ↪ ℙ¹).
377	  exact IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range _
378	```

397	## Verdicts (summary)
398	
399	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
400	|---|---|---|
401	| (1) `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul` is the right ring-level idiom | PROCEED | informational |
402	| (2) `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` IS shipped — discard "build intersection ring iso" plan | ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB | critical |
403	| (3) Outer "intersection cover X iso" still needed; pasting via Mathlib idioms | BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER | informational |
404	| (4) Chart-1 section for `iotaGm_range_isOpen` — `pullback.lift` over `pointOfVec` factorization | BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER | informational |
405	
406	## Recommendation
407	
408	**The iter-181 Lane B task_result was over-pessimistic on the upstream
409	gap.** Mathlib already ships the Spec-level intersection iso
410	`Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` with three `simp`-lemma companions
411	(`pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι`, `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst`,
412	`pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd`) that directly pin every projection. The
413	iter-182+ plan should:
414	
415	1. **Drop step 1 of the iter-181 plan** ("build `Away_X0_X1_iso`"). It's
416	   gratuitous — `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` IS the iso, packaged at the Spec
417	   level where it's needed.
418	2. **Keep step 2** ("build the source-side bridge `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1)
419	   ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`"). This is the
420	   outer pasting iso that mirrors `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` with the
421	   merged generator `X 0 * X 1`. The key step inside the pasting chain
422	   is `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (no longer a sorry).
423	3. **For step 3** (the ring-level identity `λ · u = (1/t) · λ`), use
424	   `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `_inv_snd` to push both sides of
425	   the cocycle through the intersection iso, then reduce to a
426	   `congrArg Spec.map` on the underlying ring-level equation. The
427	   residual algebra (`t · u = 1` in the merged Localization.Away) is
428	   `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf` (≤5 LOC).
429	4. **For `iotaGm_range_isOpen`** (Route 3), use the same chart-1
430	   factorization machinery the iter-181 task_result correctly identified
431	   (`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` + `pullback.lift` + the
432	   `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` reduction). This is project-side
433	   `BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER` work but does NOT depend on the intersection
434	   ring — only on chart-1 of the cover.
435	
436	**Bonus** (out of scope for iter-182's helper budget but ~30-40 LOC):
437	the same chart-1 factorization (via `r_0` instead of `r_1`, since
438	`zeroPt`'s `v 1 = 1` is the chart-1 generator) closes
439	`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` (L420 of `GmScaling.lean`).
440

442	
443	- `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` choice — already canonical.
444	- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` + `_algebraMap` — already axiom-clean
445	  iter-174 and reused in the new cross01 body recipe.
446	- `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` — already axiom-clean iter-173 and reusable
447	  uniform-in-`f` for the `X 0 * X 1` generator (degree 2 instance via
448	  `MvPolynomial.IsHomogeneous.mul`).
449	- `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` — axiom-clean iter-167; consumed by
450	  the chart maps that the cross01 body unfolds in step (a) of Recipe 2.
451	
452	## Reversal trigger
453	
454	If the `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` body of Recipe 1 cannot
455	be made to type-check at the `pullback.congrHom`/`pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι`
456	step (the `Spec.map` of the merged generator and the merged `awayι`
457	endpoint must defeq-align), iter-183 falls back to **two separate
458	isos** — `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≅ pullback q (awayι (X 0 * X 1))`
459	and `pullback q (awayι (X 0 * X 1)) ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗ GmRing)`
460	— rather than fusing them into one chained iso. Same total LOC; less
461	risk of unification timeouts.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61	
62	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
64	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
65	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
66	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
67	
68	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
69	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
70	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
71	
72	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
73	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)

87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :

144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
259	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
260	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
261	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
262	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
263	  intro y
264	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
265	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
266	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
267	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
268	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
269	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
270	    rw [htop]; trivial
271	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
272	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
273	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
274	  · -- mem case
275	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
276	    have hgen :=
277	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
278	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
279	    rw [hgen]
280	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
281	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
282	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
283	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
284	    rw [map_pow, hX]
285	  · -- algebraMap case
286	    intro r
287	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
288	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
289	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
290	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
291	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
292	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
293	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
294	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
295	    rw [hr₀]
296	    rfl
297	  · -- add case
298	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
299	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
300	  · -- mul case
301	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
302	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩

304	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
305	
306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
307	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
308	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
309	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
310	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl

161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by

324:def affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan {ι : Type*} (f : ι → A) {m : ι → ℕ}
340:  affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜

320	open TopologicalSpace.Opens in
321	/-- Given a family of homogeneous elements `f` of positive degree that spans the irrelevant ideal,
322	`Spec (A_f)₀ ⟶ Proj A` forms an affine open cover of `Proj A`. -/
323	noncomputable
324	def affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan {ι : Type*} (f : ι → A) {m : ι → ℕ}
325	    (f_deg : ∀ i, f i ∈ 𝒜 (m i)) (hm : ∀ i, 0 < m i)
326	    (hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal ≤ Ideal.span (Set.range f)) :
327	    (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover where
328	  I₀ := ι
329	  X i := .of (Away 𝒜 (f i))
330	  f i := awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)
331	  idx x := (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose
332	  covers x := by
333	    change x ∈ (awayι 𝒜 _ _ _).opensRange
334	    rw [opensRange_awayι]
335	    exact (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose_spec
336	
337	/-- `Proj A` is covered by `Spec (A_f)₀` for all homogeneous elements of positive degree. -/
338	@[simps! f] noncomputable
339	def affineOpenCover : (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover :=
340	  affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜
341	    (ι := Σ i : PNat, 𝒜 i) (m := fun i ↦ i.1) (fun i ↦ i.2) (fun i ↦ i.2.2) (fun i ↦ i.1.2) <| by
342	  classical
343	  intro z hz
344	  rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose 𝒜 z]
345	  refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun c hc ↦ if hc0 : c = 0 then ?_ else
346	    Ideal.subset_span ⟨⟨⟨c, Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hc0⟩, _⟩, rfl⟩
347	  convert Ideal.zero_mem _
348	  subst hc0
349	  exact hz
350	
351	end basicOpen
352	
353	section stalk
354	
355	/-- The stalk of `Proj A` at `x` is the degree `0` part of the localization of `A` at `x`. -/
356	noncomputable
357	def stalkIso (x : Proj 𝒜) :
358	    (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.stalk x ≅ .of (AtPrime 𝒜 x.asHomogeneousIdeal.toIdeal) :=
359	  (stalkIso' 𝒜 x).toCommRingCatIso
360	
361	end stalk

10	/-!
11	# Associativity of pullbacks
12	
13	This file shows that pullbacks (and pushouts) are associative up to natural isomorphism.
14	
15	-/
16	
17	@[expose] public section
18	
19	noncomputable section

21	open CategoryTheory
22	
23	universe w v₁ v₂ v u u₂

27	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]

29	section PullbackAssoc
30	
31	/-
32	The objects and morphisms are as follows:
33	```
34	           Z₂ - g₄ -> X₃
35	           |          |
36	           g₃         f₄
37	           ∨          ∨
38	Z₁ - g₂ -> X₂ - f₃ -> Y₂
39	|          |
40	g₁         f₂
41	∨          ∨
42	X₁ - f₁ -> Y₁
43	```
44	
45	where the two squares are pullbacks.
46	
47	We can then construct the pullback squares
48	
49	```
50	W  - l₂ -> Z₂ - g₄ -> X₃
51	|                     |
52	l₁                    f₄
53	∨                     ∨
54	Z₁ - g₂ -> X₂ - f₃ -> Y₂
55	```
56	
57	and
58	
59	```
60	W' - l₂' -> Z₂
61	|           |
62	l₁'         g₃
63	∨           ∨
64	Z₁          X₂
65	|           |
66	g₁          f₂
67	∨           ∨
68	X₁ -  f₁ -> Y₁
69	```
70	
71	We will show that both `W` and `W'` are pullbacks over `g₁, g₂`, and thus we may construct a
72	canonical isomorphism between them. -/

108	def pullbackPullbackLeftIsPullback [HasPullback (g₂ ≫ f₃) f₄] : IsLimit (PullbackCone.mk l₁ l₂
109	    (show l₁ ≫ g₂ = l₂ ≫ g₃ from (pullback.lift_fst _ _ _).symm)) := by
110	  apply leftSquareIsPullback _ rfl (pullbackIsPullback f₃ f₄)
111	  simpa [PullbackCone.pasteHoriz] using PullbackCone.mkSelfIsLimit (pullbackIsPullback _ f₄)

114	def pullbackAssocIsPullback [HasPullback (g₂ ≫ f₃) f₄] :

127	def pullbackPullbackRightIsPullback [HasPullback f₁ (g₃ ≫ f₂)] :
128	    IsLimit (PullbackCone.mk l₁' l₂' (show l₁' ≫ g₂ = l₂' ≫ g₃ from pullback.lift_snd _ _ _)) := by

136	        (show l₁' ≫ g₂ ≫ f₃ = (l₂' ≫ g₄) ≫ f₄ by
137	          rw [pullback.lift_snd_assoc, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, pullback.condition])) := by
138	  simpa [PullbackCone.pasteHoriz] using pasteHorizIsPullback rfl
139	    (pullbackIsPullback f₃ f₄) (pullbackPullbackRightIsPullback _ _ _ _)

    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)

    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)

338	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) [IsIso i₁] [IsIso i₂] [IsIso i₃] :
339	    IsIso (pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂) := by
340	  refine ⟨⟨pullback.map _ _ _ _ (inv i₁) (inv i₂) (inv i₃) ?_ ?_, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
341	  · rw [IsIso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, eq₁, IsIso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
342	  · rw [IsIso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, eq₂, IsIso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
343	  · cat_disch
344	  · cat_disch
345	
346	/-- If `f₁ = f₂` and `g₁ = g₂`, we may construct a canonical
347	isomorphism `pullback f₁ g₁ ≅ pullback f₂ g₂` -/
348	@[simps! hom]
349	def pullback.congrHom {X Y Z : C} {f₁ f₂ : X ⟶ Z} {g₁ g₂ : Y ⟶ Z} (h₁ : f₁ = f₂) (h₂ : g₁ = g₂)
350	    [HasPullback f₁ g₁] [HasPullback f₂ g₂] : pullback f₁ g₁ ≅ pullback f₂ g₂ :=
351	  asIso <| pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp [h₁]) (by simp [h₂])
352	
353	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
354	@[simp]

input.new_string:
/-- **Intersection-cover X iso** (iter-182 Lane B helper per
`analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 3 Recipe 1).

Identifies the pullback over the cross chart `(cover.f 0) ⨯ (cover.f 1)` with
`Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`, mirroring `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
with the merged generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2.

The body is a chain of iso steps:
1. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso q awayι_1 (cover.f 0)`: paste the outer pullback
   into a left-side composition `pullback (cover.f 0 ≫ q) awayι_1`.
2. `pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl`: rewrite `cover.f 0 ≫ q` as
   `pullback.snd q awayι_0 ≫ awayι_0`.
3. `(pullbackRightPullbackFstIso awayι_0 awayι_1 (pullback.snd q awayι_0)).symm`:
   bring the outer pullback into `pullback (pullback.snd q awayι_0) (pullback.fst awayι_0 awayι_1)`.
4. `pullback.map` with `i₂ = (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso ...).hom`: replace the inner
   pullback `pullback awayι_0 awayι_1 ≅ Spec (Away (X 0 * X 1))` (via
   `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`), with the new "fst" being
   `Spec.map (awayMap (X 1 hom) rfl)` (via `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left`).
5. `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso q awayι_0 (Spec.map (awayMap _ _))`: collapse the
   outer pullback to `pullback q (Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0)`.
6. `pullback.congrHom rfl (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι ...)`: identify
   `Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0 = awayι_(X_0 * X_1)`.
7. Apply the standard recipe (mirror of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`) at the merged
   generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2: `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`,
   `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (m = 2), `pullbackSpecIso`. -/
private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso
    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    pullback ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≅
      Spec (CommRingCat.of
        (TensorProduct kbar
          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
            ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1))
          (GmRing kbar))) := by
  refine pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
    pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl ≪≫
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).symm ≪≫ ?_
  refine asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _)
    (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
      rfl).hom (𝟙 _)
    (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp])
    (by
      rw [Category.comp_id]
      exact (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left
        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ _ _ _).symm)) ≪≫ ?_
  refine pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso _ _ _ ≪≫ ?_
  refine pullback.congrHom rfl
    (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1) rfl) ≪≫ ?_
  exact pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
    pullback.congrHom
      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 2) (by norm_num)
        ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1)
        ((MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0).mul (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1)))
      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
